$doc: The WRITE half of the seam. Every write the UI performs is a COMMAND — an intent verb, never an in-place record edit. The UI fires `window.commands.run(name, input)`; the host applies it generically by `kind` and appends it to the append-only `events.json`. This file IS the write contract: each entry maps 1:1 to a rune command verb + input DTO, and its `kind` seeds rune:data's immutability strategy (below the waist — the UI never knows or cares which the backend picks).

$kinds.create: append a brand-new object to its type's collection (auto-id) → rune: already-immutable (fresh ids)
$kinds.set: record a new value for fields of an existing object → rune:data decides append-child vs aggregate vs overwrite-justified, BELOW the contract
$kinds.append: push a child onto a collection field (needs `field`) → rune: append-child (history matters)
$kinds.adjust: atomically move a numeric field by `input.by` (needs `field`) → rune: aggregate (a derived counter)
$kinds.remove: tombstone an object by id → rune: a remove command + retention policy

task.create.does: Add a new task to a project.

task.setStatus.does: Move a task to a new status.
